title: Recognizing and commemorating the contributions of contemporary Latinas in the State of Illinois.
plain_summary: This bill is about celebrating Latina women who live in Illinois and the good things they have done for their communities. It says that Illinois is a good example for other states because they have elected many Latina women to government jobs. However, the bill also points out that across the whole country, there are still not enough Latina women in government compared to how many Latina people live in America. The bill wants people to recognize how important Latina women are to making our democracy work better.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes and celebrates the contributions of contemporary Latinas in the State of Illinois.

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: The United States celebrates National Women's History Month every March to honor women's contributions throughout history and today. Latinas add to the cultural, economic, and political strength of the United States. About thirty-two million two hundred forty-four thousand two hundred fourteen Latinas live in the United States, and one million one hundred sixty-five thousand three hundred twelve Latinas live in Illinois. However, Latinas are greatly underrepresented in elected office. While Latinas make up nine point six percent of the US population, they hold only two point six percent of state elected positions and three point six percent of federal offices across the country.

sections.title: Illinois as a Model State
sections.text: The State of Illinois serves as a model for supporting and electing Latinas. Illinois has greatly increased the representation of Latinas at all levels of government. This includes electing diverse and powerful Latinas in Illinois' Third Congressional District. These elected officials include village clerks, trustees, council members, alderpersons (city council members), water district commissioners, state representatives, and state senators. Many of these Latinas are the first to hold their positions or the first Latinas to serve in their communities.

sections.title: Accomplishments and Impact
sections.text: The work of Latinas at all levels of government in Illinois has led to important achievements. They have helped write women's rights into law and expanded health care for undocumented people. The grassroots organizing and political participation of Latinas resulted in electing the first Latina from the Midwest to Congress. The voices, lived experiences, and contributions of Latinas in Illinois continue to have long-lasting positive impacts for the communities they serve. Having Latinas represent Latinas is necessary to build a multicultural movement that includes people of all generations and recognizes our shared humanity.

sections.title: Progress and Future Work
sections.text: Significant progress has been made to increase the representation of Latinas in all decision-making spaces. However, there is still work that needs to be done until Latinas achieve fair representation in all sectors, industries, and leadership roles.

sections.title: House of Representatives Resolution
sections.text: Therefore, the House of Representatives celebrates the impact and contributions of contemporary Latinas in the State of Illinois. The House acknowledges the value of Latinas and the essential role they play in this nation's multicultural democracy that includes people of all generations. The House recognizes the importance of accurate and timely data about Latinas living in the United States and the role that the United States Census Bureau plays in delivering data that impacts the political and economic power and influence of Latinas.
